rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' et al VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn For Defense Costs & Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 19, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINSTRATION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS, RODERICK HICKMAN, BRIGID HANSON AND TIM VIRGA'S MOTION For Defense Costs AND Attorney'S Fees IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO RECOVER $370,529.29 FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS REASONABLY INCURRED. THE PURPOSE OF C.C.P. SECTION 1038 IS "TO DISCOURAGE FRIVILOUS LAWSUITS BY ALLOWING BLAMELESS PUBLIC ENTITIES TO RECOVER THEIR DEFENSE COSTS." HALL V REGENTS UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (1996) 43 CAL APP 4TH 1580, 1586. ACCORDINGLY, IT MAKES SENSE TO APPLY THIS STATUTE TO THE FEDERAL CLAIM UNDER SECTION 1983. DEFENDANT'S HAVE DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF LACKED REASONABLE CAUSE TO BELIEVE IN THE CLAIMS FILED AND HAVE PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF A LACK OF GOOD FAITH. UNDER SECTION 1983, THERE WAS NO BASIS TO NAME THE STATE. WILL V MICHIGAN DEPARTMENT OF STATE POLICE (1989) 491 U.S. 58, 64. FURTHER, THE REFORMS AT ISSUE WERE NEVER IMPLEMENTED AND SO THERE WAS NO REASON TO FILE SUIT AGAINST ANYONE. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM OF THE NEED TO SEEK PROSPECTIVE RELIEF IS WITHOUT MERIT. FINALLY, THE ONLY DISCOVERY TAKEN BY PLAINTIFF INDICATES THAT IT WAS DONE FOR AN IMPROPER PURPOSE. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH KINNARD VS. PAT'S LIEN SERVICE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Petition For Relief From The Claim Presentation Requirement Government Code 945.4 Allowing Filing Of Attached Complaint Against Calif Dmv Named Herein As Doe 1; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 19, 2008 LINE 5. PLAINTIFF KENNETH KINNARD'S (Amended) Petition For Relief From The Claim Presentation Requirement Government Code 945.4 Allowing Filing Of Attached Complaint Against CALIFORINA D.M.V, Named Herein As Doe 1. (Request For Judicial Notice) IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM ACCRUED WHEN PLAINTIFF DISCOVERED THE TRAILER WAS SOLD. THEREAFTER, PLAINTIFF WAS REQUIRED TO FILE A LATE CLAIM APPLICATION WITHIN ONE YEAR AFTER THE ACCRUAL OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. THIS IS A JURISDICTIONAL REQUIREMENT THAT PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JERALD J. TISHKOFF VS. ALLEN TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 19, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANT ALLEN TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof by demonstrating that the contract needed to be in writing and signed by the parties. Plaintiff's evidence fails to create a triable issue of fact. The second cause of action fails based on Freeman & Mills Inc. v. Belcher Oil Co. (1995) 11 Cal.4th 85, 88. As an experienced business person, reliance on a conditional offer is neither reasonable nor forseeable. Laks v. Coast Fed. Sav. & Loan Ass'n (1976) 60 Cal.App.3d 885, 893. The lack of mutual asset as reflected in the lack of a signed contract precludes plaintiff's claim of breach of an implied contract. As a non-resident of California, plaintiff may not relay on Labor Code sections 201 and 2802 to recover for work done in Ohio. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LORI REZABEK-KELLS et al VS. PILATUS AIRCRAFT, LTD. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest, Or In The Alterantive To Continue Gg Aircraft, Llc'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 19, 2008 LINE 10. DEFENDANT PRATT & WHITNEY CANADA CORP.'S Motion To Contest, Or In The Alternative To Continue G.G. Aircraft, L.L.C.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement: Hearing required. The parties are to address the following issues: What evidence is there of the probable economic loss of the plaintiffs? Although there does not appear to be a case directly on point, isn't it consistent with the general policy of encouraging settlements to protect settling parties who may not have been named in the case? Pratt Whitney suggests that it has no liability based on the report of the Costa Rica government and neither plaintiffs nor the settling defendants or prospective defendants have presented evidence to the contrary. If that is so, does not that support plaintiffs' desire to settle and obviate Pratt Whitney's concern of being disproportionately liable for the loss? How much of the Pratt Whitney verdicts are based on non economic damages? If the pilot's estate has $7.5 million in assets, why have not the plaintiffs named the estate of the pilot? =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SF WHARF ENTERPRISES, INC., FORMERLY, DBA SAN VS. W.W. WHARF GL, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MARCH 19, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANT JEFFREY LOWENTHAL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MAINTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN BY DEMONSTRATING THAT THIS ACTION ARISING FROM PETITIONING ACTIVITY, CONDUCT IN THE COURSE OF PRIOR LITIGATION. TO THE EXTENT, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM SOUNDS IN TORT, C.C. 47 BARS THE CLAIM. TO THE EXTENT IT SOUNDS IN CONTRACT, IT MAY WELL BE BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE AND IT IS BARRED BY COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. PLAINTIFF'S BRIEF ON APPEAL OF THE PRIOR CASE MAKES CLEAR THAT THE VERY ISSUE PRESENTED BY THIS COMPLAINT WAS LITIGATED IN THE PRIOR CASE AND A FINAL JUDGMENT HAS BEEN ENTERED IN THE PRIOR CASE REJECTING PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTIONS. =(302/PJM/PB)
